recalls.Description: The recall affects Coleman Converta camping cots and suspension stretchers, specifically model numbers 2000003077, 2000020282, and 2000020290. These items feature tan or white fabric displaying the Coleman logo and are supported by black steel frames with adjustable components. Model numbers can be found on a hang tag attached to the frame. The products may pose serious risks due to a backrest fold mechanism that can pinch fingers, leading to laceration and amputation hazards. Consumers are urged to stop using these items immediately.
recalls.Title: Coleman Converta Camping Cots and Suspension Stretchers Recalled Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact The Coleman Company at 800-345-3278 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://recall.coleman.com/ to check if your model is affected and to request a free repair kit.

Images.Caption: Recalled Coleman Converta camping suspension stretcher

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of the seatback adjustment mechanism pinching consumers' fingers, including two reports of fractured fingers.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and various retailers nationwide, as well as online on Amazon, from January 2011 through March 2025, priced between $60 and $80.

Importers.Name: Newell Brands Inc., an affiliate of The Coleman Company, Inc., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Pinching hazard from backrest fold mechanism

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit with installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Coleman recalls Converta camping cots and stretchers due to injury risks. Check if your model is affected and get a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: Cinmar is recalling all Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaises (SKU 67333) and Newport Teak Chaises (SKU 166218) due to a serious safety concern. These outdoor lounge chairs, available in various colors and finishes, feature an adjustable backrest that can unexpectedly drop while in use, posing a risk of finger crushing or amputation. The chaises measure approximately 51 inches in length and 31 inches in width, and are designed for reclining with a mesh backrest.
recalls.Title: Cinmar Recalls Frontgate Chaise Lounge Chairs Due to Risk of Finger Injury
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Cinmar at 888-298-4651, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET and Saturday from 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. Alternatively, visit www.frontgate.com/newportchaiserecall or navigate to the "Product Recalls" section on www.frontgate.com for further details.

Products.Name: Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaises

Images.Caption: ecalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaise
Recalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Teak Chaise

Injuries.Name: Cinmar has received three reports of incidents involving consumers' fingers becoming entrapped between the adjustable backrest and the chair frame, resulting in two partial finger amputation injuries, and one finger-crushing injury requiring surgery.

Retailers.Name: Available at Frontgate retail locations in Georgia, Kentucky, North Carolina, Ohio, and Texas, as well as online at Frontgate.com and in Frontgate catalogs from February 2014 through December 2023, priced between $600 and $1,400.

Importers.Name: Cinmar LLC, of West Chester, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of finger crushing and amputation from backrest adjustment

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including new backrest support bar and assembly instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Cinmar recalls Frontgate Chaise Lounge Chairs due to finger crushing hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: TJX has announced a recall for foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs, which were sold as part of three- or five-piece sets under the Nautica and House & Garden brands. These sets include a small wooden table and two to four foldable chairs featuring wood frames and rope seats and backs in various colors, supported by a gold bar at the back legs. Some chairs were also available for individual purchase at retail locations. Additionally, the chairs were marketed online as handcrafted and as part of the HD Furniture Group indoor-outdoor bistro sets.
recalls.Title: TJX Issues Recall for Foldable Wood and Rope Bistro Set Chairs Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach TJX at 888-520-0157 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/woodandropechair. Customers can also check product recalls on the respective websites of HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, and Sierra.

Products.Name: Foldable Wood and Rope Bistro Set Chairs

Images.Caption: Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (blue)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (gray)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (sand)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated in the chair, resulting in six reports of minor injuries, including bruising and an ankle strain.

Retailers.Name: Available at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods, Homesense, and Sierra stores nationwide, as well as online at Homegoods.com, TJmaxx.tjx.com, Marshalls.com, and Sierra.com from March 2020 through September 2023, priced between $130 and $350 for sets and $40 to $80 for individual chairs.

Importers.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage or collapse of the wooden chair frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled chairs immediately and contact TJX for a full refund and disposal instructions. Alternatively, chairs can be returned to any HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, or Sierra store for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: TJX recalls foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs due to fall hazards. Immediate action recommended for consumers.
